item.title: Nonprofit Audit Fundamentals: A Practical Guide for Nonprofits
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn best practices for auditing nonprofit financials—what to prepare, common challenges, and how to stay compliant and audit-ready year-round.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
In an uncertain economy, nonprofits must do more with less, with internal and external stakeholders paying closer attention to every dollar. That’s why audit readiness isn’t just a compliance exercise; it’s a powerful tool for building trust and long-term sustainability.

A well-prepared audit can help your nonprofit:

  • Demonstrate responsible stewardship of donor and grant funds
  • Strengthen confidence among funders and supporters
  • Equip your board and leadership with critical financial insights
Preparing for an audit is year-round, and the Nonprofit Audit Fundamentals guide is here to help. Discover how to:

Reframe your organization’s view on audit season. Download the guide today and help your nonprofit stay resilient, transparent, and audit ready.

item.title: Prioritizing the attendee experience
item.dc:creator: dev@500designs.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Download our guide to Future-proof your conferences and events by really listening to your attendees

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Download our guide to Future-proof your conferences and events by really listening to your attendees

Future-proof your conferences and events by really listening to your attendees 

Momentive Event Management surveyed event planners and other organizers in collaboration with BizBash. These findings provide insight into how prioritizing attendees is tied to the success of events and conferences.  

Actively gathering and acting on attendee feedback—through surveys, social listening, and real-time polling—helps organizers and event planners achieve success.  

This attendee-centric approach not only fosters trust but also enables conferences and trade shows to adapt to shifting industry dynamics, budget constraints, and evolving audience expectations.
This data-rich guide shares more on:

Everything you need to govern, scale, and productize AI across your client portfolio

Many Association Management Companies (AMCs) are experimenting with AI, but only a few know how to use it effectively across all their client portfolios. 

This guide is for AMCs ready to go beyond small wins and random tool tests. It offers a clear, step-by-step path from getting started to delivering real strategic value, all while maintaining client trust, confidentiality, and your brand voice.

item.title: Finding The Passion: Empowering Mission-Driven Employees in 2026
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Burnout is driving turnover across mission-driven teams. Download the 2026 report for data-backed workforce retention insights.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Burnout is driving turnover across mission-driven teams. Download the 2026 report for data-backed workforce retention insights.

For years, the "passion tax" has relied on the incredible heart staff bring to their work, but rising burnout is now driving nearly 60% of mission-driven employees to seek new opportunities. 

Instead of relying on altruism alone, forward-thinking organizations must address the technological inefficiencies and unclear growth paths that are actively fueling this turnover. This report analyzes the current state of the workforce to help you move from a culture of burnout to sustainable empowerment. 

Empowering your workforce requires equipping them with the tools to succeed in an AI-ready future while strengthening their "line of sight" to your organization's mission.

Download Finding The Passion: Empowering Mission-Driven Employees in 2026 today to uncover the priorities, concerns, and data-driven strategies you need to reignite passion and secure your team's future.

item.title: Closing the Gap: Solving the Disconnect Around Career Prioritization
item.dc:creator: colleen.bernum@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Membership lapse is rising. Our 2025 report reveals the gap between association priorities and member demands for career services. Download the data and solution.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
For years, a critical knowledge gap has plagued associations, leading directly to membership lapse. Associations underprioritize career services, yet members see them as a top priority.

This report looks at the persistent gap between association offerings and member values, documents both current and lapsed member priorities and explores why a one-size-fits-all approach is fueling churn across all professional stages.

Want to stop the lapse and become a trusted career authority for your members? Download this guide today and learn how to close the career services gap by turning your programs into core membership value that strengthens loyalty and ensures sustainability.

item.excerpt:encoded: Enhance your membership retention strategies, while specifically targeting Gen Z preferences.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Understanding Association Lapser Risk by Generation
Enhance your membership retention strategies across all generations, while specifically targeting Gen Z preferences.

Enhance your membership retention strategies, while specifically targeting Gen Z preferences. 

Associations today face rapidly evolving membership landscapes. Generational expectations, communication styles, and the very definition of value are shifting faster than ever before. 

Younger members, particularly Gen Z, show some of the highest levels of engagement, satisfaction, and enthusiasm, while also representing the greatest lapser risk.  

Read our guide to discover how to elevate your member experience and strengthen retention across all age groups, especially among those most at risk of lapsing--Gen Z.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Protect your mission & donor trust. Learn how to implement internal controls, segregate duties & leverage technology to prevent fraud and stay audit-ready.

For mission-driven organizations, every dollar represents a life changed, a community served, or a cause championed. But without a clear system of internal controls, even the most passionate teams are vulnerable to fraud, costly audit failures, and a loss of donor trust. 

You don't need a massive finance department to build a secure foundation. Whether you are a team of two or two hundred, our latest guide breaks down the essential steps to safeguard your finances and keep your focus where it belongs: on your mission.

item.title: Top 10 Tips to Avoid Common AMS Buying Pitfalls
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to avoid common AMS buying pitfalls with practical tips to guide a smarter, more confident association software selection process.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Top 10 Tips to Avoid Common AMS Buying Pitfalls
Learn how to avoid common AMS buying pitfalls with practical tips to guide a smarter, more confident association software selection process.

Choosing a new Association Management System (AMS) is a pivotal decision. It's more than a technology purchase; it shapes your association for years. Get it right, and you advance; get it wrong, and it could have real consequences for membership and engagement.   

Most failed implementations arise from rushed evaluations, misaligned stakeholders, hidden costs, or vendors impressive in demos but unproven in practice.   

This tip sheet provides actionable steps to help your team select an AMS that improves efficiency, reduces unnecessary expenses, and promotes alignment across departments.
What you'll walk away knowing how to do:

item.title: Best Practices for Membership Model Pricing
item.dc:creator: soniya.bajoria@communitybrands.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover membership pricing strategy models that help associations & nonprofits set the right dues, grow revenue, & retain more members.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover membership pricing strategy models that help associations & nonprofits set the right dues, grow revenue, & retain more members.

How to set the right price for every membership level

Getting membership pricing right is one of the most important decisions your association will make. Price too high and you risk turning prospective members away; price too low and you undercut the perceived value of everything your organization offers. The good news: with the right framework, you can find the sweet spot that supports both your mission and your revenue goals.

item.title: Driving Non-Dues Revenue with Online Stores
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Non-dues revenue helps associations offset membership declines. Learn how an online store powered by your AMS creates sustainable, year-round income.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Grow Non-Dues Revenue with Online Stores
Non-dues revenue helps associations offset membership declines. Learn how an online store powered by your AMS creates sustainable, year-round income.

Non-dues revenue is an essential aspect of any association’s financial health, and in today’s economic climate, it’s even more paramount. As your association goes through periods of flat or declining memberships, non-dues revenue helps bridge the gap, making up losses and ensuring your association doesn’t have to scale back services. 

A key driver of non-member revenue is your association’s online store. A thriving e-commerce strategy can help your association grow sustainably, and the right association management software (AMS) gives associations the tools to implement an e-commerce strategy quickly.
Let’s explore how an online store solves key challenges and creates new opportunities for your association.

item.title: How small staff associations can reach members faster with SMS
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to improve member communication with SMS. Discover strategies to boost engagement, send timely updates, & increase response rates.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Member Communication with SMS for Associations
Learn how to improve member communication with SMS. Discover strategies to boost engagement, send timely updates, & increase response rates.

Tips and templates to boost engagement and simplify communication  

For small staff associations, getting members to see and act on your messages can feel like an uphill battle, especially with crowded inboxes and overflowing feeds. That’s where SMS makes all the difference.  

Text messaging boasts a 98% open rate, compared to just 20% for email, and 95% of SMS messages are read within three minutes of delivery. It’s the perfect channel to get your message in front of your members instantly.
In this free guide, you’ll learn:

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:75:"Why SMS is the fastest and most effective member communication channel
";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:83:"Proven tips to launch successful campaigns — from reminders to event invites
";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:66:"Six easy-to-use templates to simplify your messaging strategy
";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:61:"Best practices for opt-in compliance, targeting, and content ";}}
Whether you’re promoting events, driving renewals, or sharing valuable updates, SMS helps your messages land — and get noticed — faster.

item.title: The small association’s guide to getting started with an online community
item.dc:creator: meera.parmar@momentivestg.wpenginepowered.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Learn how to build a strong online community with proven strategies to increase engagement, participation, & long-term member value.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn how to build a strong online community with proven strategies to increase engagement, participation, & long-term member value.
Guide to Building an Online Community

Give members more of the benefits they want to drive greater member engagement and loyalty 

Your organization needs to connect members to events, volunteer opportunities, education, jobs, and industry information. An online community is the ideal vehicle to deliver these types of benefits to your members. It gives your members the tools they need throughout their member journey to learn, share ideas, and become loyal advocates – ultimately driving retention, revenue, and member satisfaction. 

So why haven’t you launched an online community yet? Maybe you aren’t sure how to get started. Or maybe your small staff feels too strapped for time to manage one more thing. 

No matter what’s been holding you back, there’s good news. Launching an online community is easier than you think.
This guide offers insights into four key areas to help you get started:

a:4:{s:6:"item-0";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:55:"Ideas for what you can do with an online community
";}s:6:"item-1";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:51:"Simple steps for launching an online community
";}s:6:"item-2";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:60:"Best practices for building a thriving online community
";}s:6:"item-3";a:1:{s:11:"description";s:56:"Tips on what to look for in online community technology ";}}
Download this guide today and get started with an online community.
